data.title: STOCKS | Jefferies Raises AMD Price Target to $640 From $515
data.description: Jefferies raised its price target on AMD (AMD.O) to $640 from $515. According to Jin10, the update was reported by Jefferies.

data.title: Cathay Pacific to Adjust Passenger and Cargo Fuel Surcharges Starting August 1
data.description: According to Jin10, Cathay Pacific said it will adjust passenger and cargo fuel surcharges for Cathay Pacific and Cathay Cargo starting August 1 in response to the recent rise in fuel prices.

data.title: Dangote Cement Prefers London for Unit Listing
data.description: Dangote Cement Plc prefers London over Dubai for the planned listing of its cement business because shares can be sold more quickly on the UK exchange, according to Mariya Dangote, a board member in the unit, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: India's July Preliminary Manufacturing PMI Falls to 53.9
data.description: India's July preliminary manufacturing PMI came in at 53.9, below the expected 54.7 and the previous 54.2. According to Jin10, the reading was released as an initial estimate.

data.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $65,400 as Magnificent Seven Stocks Lose $797 Billion
data.description: Bitcoin traded near $65,400 in Friday Asian trading as U.S. megacap technology stocks weakened on Thursday. According to NS3.AI, the Magnificent Seven lost about $797 billion in market value, pulling the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 lower. Bitcoin was down less than 1% on the day and up 3% on the week.

data.title: Boeing and Union Reach Preliminary Deal on Four Non-Economic Contract Terms
data.description: According to Jin10, the International Federation of Professional and Technical Engineers said it has reached a preliminary agreement with Boeing on four non-economic contract terms. The union team and Boeing also continued earlier discussions on economic issues, including pay and retirement benefits.

data.title: Hyundai Motor Union Plans Next Week's Partial Strikes Over Wages
data.description:
According to Yonhap, Hyundai Motor Co.'s labor union said Friday it will extend partial strikes next week as workers press for higher wages, a move that could disrupt production at South Korea's largest automaker. The union said employees on day and night shifts already staged four-hour walkouts from Monday through Wednesday this week after wage talks stalled, and it plans another round of four-hour strikes from Wednesday to next Friday, which would be its third walkout this year.

The two sides have held 15 rounds of negotiations without narrowing their differences. The 40,000-member union is seeking a 149,600-won increase in monthly base pay and a performance bonus equal to 30% of last year's net profit, while Hyundai Motor, the maker of Sonata sedans and Palisade sport utility vehicles, has offered an 80,000-won raise, a bonus equivalent to 350% of monthly salary plus 10 million won and 15 shares of company stock. Hyundai Motor posted net profit of 10.36 trillion won ($7.07 billion) last year.

data.title: STOCKS | JPMorgan Raises Intel Price Target to $85 From $45
data.description: JPMorgan raised its price target on Intel (INTC.O) to $85 from $45. According to Odaily, the update was reported in a brief market note.

data.title: JPMorgan Moves 30+ Quant Analysts From Mainland China to Singapore and Hong Kong
data.description: People familiar with the matter said JPMorgan Chase has relocated more than 30 quantitative analysts from mainland China to Singapore and Hong Kong in recent months to centralize the team in regional hubs and bring it closer to business units, according to 36Kr. Earlier this year, JPMorgan also set up a quantitative trading and research team to accelerate its electronic trading business and respond to competition from non-bank rivals. Market makers including Citadel Securities and Jane Street Group LLC have expanded rapidly in recent years on the back of electronic trading.

data.title: Global Bonds Reeling as Oil Surge Revives Inflation Concerns
data.description:
Global bonds are being pummeled by the latest surge in energy prices, erasing gains for investors who had bet this year’s rout was over, according to Bloomberg.

The move is setting up a credibility test for central bankers as inflation risks re-emerge.

data.title: Wang Bi Prefers Berkshire Over Apple Amid AI Capex Debate
data.description:
Wang Bi, dean of the Hong Kong Austrian Economic School, said he is not fond of the heavy capital spending by large technology companies, but remains optimistic about Google's full ecosystem from chips to consumers as markets question whether big AI infrastructure investment will generate enough returns, according to Ming Pao.

He noted that Alphabet's quarterly results were strong, but concerns rose after cash flow turned negative. Wang said Google's share price may stay subdued in the near term because of excess spending, but he sees the company building a deep moat and plans to add to the stock gradually through sell puts around a $290 strike price, with an after-premium cost of about $270.

On Apple, Wang said the stock has recently hit a record high and that its smaller capital needs make it more resilient and a favorite of Warren Buffett. Still, in a comparison between Apple and Berkshire Hathaway, Wang said he favors Berkshire for the long term because Apple keeps buying back shares at any price, while Berkshire only acts when shares trade below intrinsic value. He said he has sold U.S. Treasurys and rotated into Berkshire amid concerns over U.S. debt and inflation.

data.title: Polymarket's Musk Tweet Count Submarket Sees Sharp Odds Drop
data.description: A submarket in Polymarket’s "Elon Musk tweet count prediction" event, labeled "180-199," saw its "Yes" probability fall sharply from 30.5% one hour earlier to 11.5%, a 19% swing. According to ChainCatcher, the move reflects a sudden shift in the market’s expectations and may be related to a breaking news event.

data.title: Hanwha Ocean Signs U.S. Deals On Naval Ship Design, Supply Chain
data.description:
According to Yonhap, Hanwha Ocean Co., the shipbuilding arm of South Korea's Hanwha Group, said Friday it signed multiple agreements with U.S. companies on naval ship design and shipbuilding supply chain development. The deals were signed in Washington on Thursday during the opening ceremony of the Korea-U.S. Shipbuilding Partnership Center, which was set up to support the "Make American Shipbuilding Great Again" initiative aimed at reviving the U.S. shipbuilding industry.

Hanwha Ocean said it signed a professional services agreement with Leidos Gibbs & Cox for the design of the Global Fast Sealift vessel, following an April memorandum of understanding on next-generation naval ship design and expanding U.S. shipbuilding capacity. It also signed an MOU with Hanwha Philly Shipyard, a U.S. subsidiary of Hanwha Group, and Delaware County Community College on shipbuilding technology training and workforce development, as well as another MOU with Hanwha Philly Shipyard, the Greater Philadelphia Chamber of Commerce and the Greater Philadelphia Growth Partnership on strengthening the supply chain.

The company said it is prepared to support the revival of the U.S. shipbuilding industry while backing the continued growth of South Korea's shipbuilding sector. Jung In-sub, president and head of the Management Support Office at Hanwha Ocean, said the company will expand cooperation in areas most critical to the U.S. and help drive the MASGA project.

data.title: STOCKS | Brent Crude Falls Near $86 After Surging Above $100, U.S. 30-Year Yield Stays Above 5%
data.description: Brent crude futures rose 7.1% intraday yesterday to break above $100 before reversing sharply, and are down nearly 14% today to around $86. The 30-year U.S. Treasury yield has stayed above 5% for 14 straight trading days, the longest stretch since 2007. According to PANews, AI demand remains, but the market is concerned about spending running too fast, while semiconductor and memory stocks have held up relatively better, defense stocks have served as a safe haven, and the seven major technology stocks lost nearly $800 billion in market value in a single day.

data.title: Iran Warns Asset Seizures Would Set Dangerous Precedent
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on July 24 that seizing a country's assets to pay for possible future claims unrelated to those assets would set a dangerous precedent and increase tensions, according to Jiemian News. Araghchi said supporters or beneficiaries of such actions should realize that if countries normalize confiscating foreign property, no assets will be safe and none will be immune. He warned the resulting chaos and instability would be “neither welcome nor peaceful.”

data.title: China's Sci-Tech 50 ETF Sees 982 Million Yuan Net Inflow Over 11 Days
data.description:
As of 11:16 a.m. on July 24, the Science and Technology Innovation Board 50 Index had mixed performance, with ACM Research up 6.37%, Aoojie Technology up 5.24%, and Cambricon up 5.13%, while Bewinner Storage led declines. Ping An Sci-Tech 50 ETF last traded at 1.29 yuan, and according to Jiemian News, it has recorded net inflows for 11 straight days, including a single-day high of 512 million yuan and a cumulative 982 million yuan.

SEMI said global semiconductor equipment sales are expected to rise 23.2% in 2026 to $165.9 billion and reach a record $229.5 billion in 2028. A CICC research note said semiconductor equipment components are benefiting from both AI-driven capacity expansion and a low domestic localization rate for key parts, with high-end localization still at an early stage.

data.title: Tsmc, Alphabet, Samsung Expand Capex as Changxin Technology Prepares Shanghai IPO
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. added $100 billion in U.S. investment and raised its full-year capital spending target to $60 billion-$64 billion, while Alphabet lifted AI capital expenditure to $195 billion-$205 billion and Samsung is reviewing a plan to double the initial scale of its Taylor, Texas plant, according to Jiemian News. SEMI said the global semiconductor equipment upcycle could last until 2028.

Nvidia also prepaid $1.5 billion to Amkor to support the expansion of its U.S. advanced packaging capacity. Amkor's advanced packaging campus in Arizona carries a total investment of $7 billion and is expected to start production in 2028.

Separately, Changxin Technology is set to list on the STAR Market on July 27, 2026, which Jiemian News said would make it the second-largest IPO in the board's history. Of the 29.5 billion yuan raised, more than 20 billion yuan will go to wafer fabrication line upgrades and DRAM research and development, while its three 12-inch fabs are targeting monthly output of 400,000 wafers. Jiemian News said the company's expansion is expected to lift demand for lithography, etching and testing equipment.

data.title: Baowu Resources Raises Registered Capital to 20.26 Billion Yuan
data.description: Baowu Resources Co. has increased its registered capital from about 17.82 billion yuan to about 20.26 billion yuan, according to Jiemian News. The corporate filing shown by Tianyancha also says the company was founded in July 2006, with Shi Bing as legal representative. Its business scope includes sales of metal ores, coal and related products, metal materials, domestic trade agency services and international cargo transport agency services. Baowu Resources is wholly owned by China Baowu Steel Group.

data.title: Mainland Regulators Question Air China, China Southern Over Ticket Overselling
data.description:
Mainland media reported that consumer associations in Beijing, Tianjin and surrounding areas launched a joint special survey on awareness of civil aviation ticket overselling. The groups held joint talks today with 10 airlines, including Air China (0753) and China Southern Airlines (1055), as well as five online ticketing platforms, including Tongcheng (0780), Trip.com (9961) and Meituan (3690), according to Ming Pao.

The companies were asked to complete their self-inspection and rectification work by the 31st of next month.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces $1.47 Billion Short Liquidations Above $68,421
data.description: Bitcoin could trigger $1.47 billion in short liquidations on major centralized exchanges if it breaks above $68,421. According to ChainCatcher, long liquidations on major CEXs would total $1.37 billion if BTC falls below $62,211.
